description_long: This object is a pair of traditional Japanese wooden sandals known as geta. Each sandal features a flat, rectangular wooden base with visible wood grain texture and two raised wooden supports underneath for elevation. The upper part consists of a dark, fabric thong strap that is anchored at three points: one at the front and two at the sides, forming a V-shape. A small, rounded wooden peg is present at the front where the strap meets the base, providing structural support. The sandals are simple in design, with no visible branding or decorative elements, and the craftsmanship highlights the natural wood and utilitarian construction. These sandals are typically worn with traditional attire and are designed for outdoor use on urban streets or walkways.
description: A pair of traditional Japanese wooden sandals (geta) with a flat wood base and dark fabric thong straps, featuring two raised supports underneath. The design is simple, highlighting natural wood grain and functional construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the V-shaped dark fabric straps and the rounded wooden pegs at the front of each sandal.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the two raised wooden supports under each sandal and the arch of the fabric straps.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back ends of the wooden bases and the continuation of the fabric straps.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again showing the raised wooden supports and the side profile of the straps.
